mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of co-occurring expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.5% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in > 45% of observations, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend indicates expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 revisions show no predictive power for 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals severe supply-side contraction and manufacturing weakness, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to maintain directional accuracy during manufacturing downturns.
